zh.lifeSummary: 當Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Ddu於1332年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根時，她的父親Llywelyn ap Gruffydd29歲，母親Margred Verch Madog Fychan39歲。她和Sir David Hanmer至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於大約1357年在威爾士福林爾享年26歲。

nameMeanings.name: Ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

variants.name: Ferg
meanings: German: occupational name for a ferryman, Middle High German verje, verge.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Llewelyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Llewellyn
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Llywelyn, derived from British Celtic Lugubelinos, itself from the divine name Lugus + an uncertain second element often used alone as a name. Through association with llew ‘lion’, it was altered to Llewelyn. This was the name of two princes of north Wales who held the Normans at bay for many years in the 13th century. Etymologically speaking, the form with a fourth -l- is an error, not found as an original Welsh name, but this is now much the most common spelling in the US.

nameMeanings.name: Ddu
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Du
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Dau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Son, Tuan, Dai, Dinh, Duong, Giao, Hoa, Huong, Khuyen, Linh, Mau, Phu.
North German, East Frisian, and North Frisian: from the Frisian personal name Douwe, from dou ‘pigeon’.
South German: from Davo, a short form of the personal name Tavold, formed with Old High German dau ‘custom, tradition’.

nameMeanings.name: Angharad
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Old Celtic intensive prefix an- + cār ‘love’ + the noun suffix -ad. This was the name of the mother of the 12th-century chronicler Giraldus Cambrensis (‘Gerald the Welshman’). In the Mabinogi, Angharad Golden Hand at first rejects Peredur's suit, but later falls in love with him when he comes back as the unknown Mute Knight. The name has been strongly revived in Wales since the 1940s, borne, for example, by the actress Angharad Rees ( 1949-2012 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Ddu was born in 1332, in Glamorgan, Wales, her father, Llywelyn ap Gruffydd, was 29 and her mother, Margred Verch Madog Fychan, was 39. She had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Sir David Hanmer. She died about 1357, in Flintshire, Wales, at the age of 26.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Ddu est née en 1332, à Glamorgan, Galles, son père, Llywelyn ap Gruffydd, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Margred Verch Madog Fychan, avait 39 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Sir David Hanmer. Elle est décédée environ 1357, à Flintshire, Galles, à l’âge de 26 ans.

Sie hatte mit Sir David Hanmer mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb um 1357 im Alter von 26 Jahren in Flintshire, Wales.

ja.lifeSummary: Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Dduが1332年にウェールズグラモーガンで生まれた時，父Llywelyn ap Gruffyddは29歳，母Margred Verch Madog Fychanは39歳であった。彼女はSir David Hanmerとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1357年にウェールズフリントシュアイアで26歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Ddu(이)가 1332년 웨일스 글램르건에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Llywelyn ap Gruffydd(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margred Verch Madog Fychan(은)는 39세였다. 그녀는 Sir David Hanmer(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1357년 웨일스 플린츠히르에서 26세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Angharad Ferch Of Llewelyn Ddu родилась в 1332 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Llywelyn ap Gruffydd было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Margred Verch Madog Fychan, 39. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Sir David Hanmer. Она умерла около 1357 г. в местности флинцхир, Уэльс в возрасте 26 лет.
